india-1-ashwagandha.title: Withania somnifera
india-1-ashwagandha.extract: Withania somnifera, known commonly as ashwagandha, is an evergreen shrub in the Solanaceae family that is native to the Middle East and North Africa, other African regions, southern Europe, Indian subcontinent, and across Southeast Asia. Several other species in the genus Withania are morphologically similar. Other common names include Indian ginseng and winter cherry.

india-2-turmeric.title: Turmeric
india-2-turmeric.extract: Turmeric, or Curcuma longa, is a flowering plant in the ginger family Zingiberaceae. It is a perennial, rhizomatous, herbaceous plant native to the Indian subcontinent and Southeast Asia that requires temperatures between 20 and 30 °C and high annual rainfall to thrive. Plants are gathered each year for their rhizomes, some for propagation in the following season and some for consumption or dyeing

india-3-triphala-amla-haritaki-bibhitaki.title: Triphala
india-3-triphala-amla-haritaki-bibhitaki.extract: Triphala is a polyherbal Ayurvedic formulation, specifically a churna or powder, produced from chebulic myrobalan (Haritaki), beleric myrobalan (Bibhitaki), and amla (Amalaki). It is one of the most trusted combinations in Ayurveda, used for gastrointestinal health and rejuvenation.

india-4-ginger.title: Ginger
india-4-ginger.description: Species of plant used as a spice
india-4-ginger.extract: Ginger is a flowering plant whose rhizome, ginger root or ginger, is widely used as a spice and a folk medicine. It is an herbaceous perennial that grows annual pseudostems about one meter tall, bearing narrow leaf blades. The inflorescences bear flowers having pale yellow petals with purple edges, and arise directly from the rhizome on separate shoots.

india-5-amla.title: Phyllanthus emblica
india-5-amla.extract: Phyllanthus emblica, also known as emblic, emblic myrobalan, myrobalan, nelikai, Indian gooseberry, Malacca tree, amloki or amla, is a deciduous tree of the family Phyllanthaceae. Its native range is tropical and southern Asia.

india-6-licorice.title: Liquorice
india-6-licorice.extract: Liquorice or licorice is the common name of Glycyrrhiza glabra, a flowering plant of the bean family Fabaceae, from the root of which a sweet, aromatic flavouring is extracted.

india-7-brahmi.title: Bacopa monnieri
india-7-brahmi.extract: Bacopa monnieri, also known as water hyssop, brahmi, thyme-leafed gratiola, herb of grace, and Indian pennywort, is a perennial, creeping herb native to wetland areas globally.

india-8-cinnamon.title: Cinnamomum verum
india-8-cinnamon.extract: Cinnamomum verum, also known as true cinnamon or Ceylon cinnamon is a small evergreen tree belonging to the family Lauraceae, native to Sri Lanka. The inner bark of the tree is historically regarded as the spice cinnamon, though this term was later generalized to include C. cassia as well.

india-9-moringa.title: Moringa oleifera
india-9-moringa.extract: Moringa oleifera is a short-lived, fast-growing, drought-resistant tree of the family Moringaceae, native to northern India and used extensively in South and Southeast Asia. Common names include moringa, drumstick tree, horseradish tree, or malunggay.

india-10-aloe-vera.title: Aloe vera
india-10-aloe-vera.extract: Aloe vera is a succulent plant species of the genus Aloe. It is widely distributed, and is considered an invasive species in many world regions.

india-11-tulsi.title: Ocimum tenuiflorum
india-11-tulsi.extract: Ocimum tenuiflorum, commonly known as tulasi, tulsi, or holy basil, is an aromatic perennial plant in the family Lamiaceae. It is widely cultivated throughout the Southeast Asian tropics. It is native to tropical and subtropical regions of Asia, Australia and the western Pacific. This plant has escaped from cultivation and has naturalized in many tropical regions of the Americas. It is an agricult

india-12-neem.title: Azadirachta indica
india-12-neem.extract: Azadirachta indica, commonly known as neem, margosa, nimtree or Indian lilac, is a tree in the mahogany family Meliaceae. It is one of the two species in the genus Azadirachta. It is native to the Indian subcontinent and to parts of Southeast Asia, but is naturalized and grown around the world in tropical and subtropical areas. Its fruits and seeds are the source of neem oil. Nim is a Hindustani n

india-13-giloy.title: Tinospora cordifolia
india-13-giloy.extract: Tinospora cordifolia, guruchi, guduchi, amrita, or the heart-leaved moonseed, is a herbaceous vine of the family Menispermaceae native to South and Southeast Asia. It has been used in Ayurveda in an attempt to treat various disorders.

india-14-shatavari.title: Asparagus racemosus
india-14-shatavari.extract: Asparagus racemosus is a species of asparagus native from Africa through southern Asia, including the Indian subcontinent, to northern Australia. It grows 1–2 m tall and prefers to take root in gravelly, rocky soils high up in piedmont plains, at 1,300–1,400 m (4,300–4,600 ft) elevation. It was botanically described in 1799.88

india-15-fenugreek.title: Fenugreek
india-15-fenugreek.extract: Fenugreek is an annual plant in the family Fabaceae, with leaves consisting of three small obovate to oblong leaflets. It is cultivated worldwide as a semiarid crop. Its leaves and seeds are common ingredients in dishes from the Indian subcontinent, and have been used as a culinary ingredient since ancient times. Its use as a food ingredient in small quantities is safe.

india-16-cumin.title: Cumin
india-16-cumin.description: Species of plant with seeds used as a spice
india-16-cumin.extract: Cumin is a flowering plant in the family Apiaceae, native to the Irano-Turanian Region. Its seeds – each one contained within a fruit, which is dried – are used in the cuisines of many cultures in both whole and ground form. Although cumin is used in traditional medicine, there is no high-quality evidence that it is safe or effective as a therapeutic agent.

india-17-coriander.title: Coriander
india-17-coriander.extract: Coriander, or Coriandrum sativum, is an annual herb in the family Apiaceae. The leaves are known as cilantro in the US. Most people perceive the leaves as having a fresh, slightly citrus taste. Due to variations in the gene OR6A2, some people perceive its flavor to be more soaplike or rotten.

india-18-cardamom.title: Elettaria cardamomum
india-18-cardamom.extract: Elettaria cardamomum, commonly known as green cardamom or true cardamom, is a herbaceous, perennial plant in the ginger family, native to southern India. It is the most common of the species whose seeds are used as a spice called cardamom that has a strong aroma used in both savory and sweet cooking. It is cultivated widely in tropical regions and reportedly naturalized in Réunion, Indochina, and

india-19-black-pepper.title: Black pepper
india-19-black-pepper.description: Ground fruit of the family Piperaceae
india-19-black-pepper.extract: Black pepper is a flowering vine in the family Piperaceae, cultivated for its fruit, which is usually dried and used as a spice and seasoning. The fruit is a drupe (stonefruit) which is about 5 mm in diameter, dark red, and contains a stone which encloses a single pepper seed. Peppercorns and the ground pepper derived from them may be described simply as pepper, or more precisely as black pepper,

india-20-long-pepper.title: Long pepper
india-20-long-pepper.description: Species of plant cultivated for use as spice
india-20-long-pepper.extract: Long pepper, sometimes called Indian long pepper or pippali, is a flowering vine in the family Piperaceae, cultivated for its fruit, which is usually dried and used as a spice and seasoning. Long pepper has a taste similar to, but sweeter and more pungent than, that of its close relative Piper nigrum – from which black, green and white pepper are obtained.

india-21-trikatu-pippali-pepper-ginger.title: Trikatu
india-21-trikatu-pippali-pepper-ginger.extract: Trikatu is a classical Ayurvedic formula combining three pungent herbs: Pippali (long pepper), Maricha (black pepper), and Sunthi (dry ginger). The name means 'three pungents' in Sanskrit. It is traditionally used to stimulate digestion and support metabolic function.

india-22-bitter-gourd.title: Momordica charantia
india-22-bitter-gourd.description: Species of plant in the gourd family
india-22-bitter-gourd.extract: Momordica charantia is a tropical and subtropical vine of the family Cucurbitaceae, widely grown in Asia, Africa, and the Caribbean for its edible fruit. Its many varieties differ substantially in the shape and bitterness of the fruit.

india-23-vasaka.title: Justicia adhatoda
india-23-vasaka.extract: Justicia adhatoda commonly known in English as Malabar nut, adulsa, adhatoda, vasa, vasaka, is native to Asia. Adathoda means 'untouched by goats' in Tamil. The name derives from the fact that animals like goats do not eat this plant due to its extreme bitter taste.

india-24-arjuna-bark.title: Terminalia arjuna
india-24-arjuna-bark.extract: Terminalia arjuna is a tree of the genus Terminalia. It is commonly known as arjuna or arjun tree in English. It is used as a traditional medicinal plant.

india-25-gokshura.title: Tribulus terrestris
india-25-gokshura.extract: Tribulus terrestris is an annual plant in the caltrop family (Zygophyllaceae) widely distributed around the world. It is adapted to thrive in dry climate locations in which few other plants can survive.

india-26-punarnava.title: Boerhavia diffusa
india-26-punarnava.extract: Boerhavia diffusa is a species of flowering plant in the four o'clock family which is commonly known as punarnava, red spiderling, spreading hogweed, or tarvine. It is taken in herbal medicine for pain relief and other uses. The leaves of Boerhavia diffusa are often used as a green vegetable in many parts of India.

india-27-kutki.title: Picrorhiza kurroa
india-27-kutki.extract: Picrorhiza kurroa is one of the major income generating non-timber forest products found in the Nepalese Himalayas. It is one of the oldest medicinal plants traded from the Karnali zone. Known as kutki or कुटकी in Nepali, it is a perennial herb and is used as a substitute for Indian gentian.

india-28-bhringraj.title: Eclipta prostrata
india-28-bhringraj.extract: Eclipta prostrata, the false daisy, is a species of plant in the family Asteraceae. It is widespread across much of the world.

india-29-jatamansi.title: Nardostachys
india-29-jatamansi.extract: Nardostachys is a genus of flowering plant in the honeysuckle family (Caprifoliaceae). Nardostachys jatamansi is the sole species in genus.

india-30-shankhpushpi.title: Convolvulus prostratus
india-30-shankhpushpi.extract: Convolvulus prostratus is an herb found in India and Burma that is used in Ayurveda. In Ayurveda it is known as shankhpushpi and the preparation shankapushpi is, according to most sources, identical with Convolvulus prostratus, but some say shankapushpi is instead Clitoria ternatea (अपराजिता). Some also say Shankhapushpi is Evolvulus alsinoides, which is another plant of the same family. It has be

india-31-vidari-kanda.title: Pueraria tuberosa
india-31-vidari-kanda.extract: Pueraria tuberosa, commonly known as kudzu, Indian kudzu, or Nepalese kudzu, Vidarikand, Sanskrit: Bhukushmandi (भूकुशमंडी) is a climber with woody tuberculated stem. It is a climbing, coiling and trailing vine with large tuberous roots. The tubers are globose or pot-like, about 25 centimetres (9.8 in) across and the insides are white, starchy and mildly sweet. Leaves are trifoliate and alternate,

india-32-kapikacchu.title: Mucuna pruriens
india-32-kapikacchu.extract: Mucuna pruriens is a tropical legume native to Africa and tropical Asia and widely naturalized and cultivated. Its English common names include monkey tamarind, velvet bean, Bengal velvet bean, Florida velvet bean, Mauritius velvet bean, Yokohama velvet bean, cowage, cowitch, lacuna bean, and Lyon bean.

india-33-guggul.title: Commiphora wightii
india-33-guggul.extract: Commiphora wightii, with common names Indian bdellium-tree, gugal, guggal, guggul, gugul, or mukul myrrh tree, is a flowering plant in the family Burseraceae, which produces a fragrant resin called gugal, guggul or gugul, that is used in incense and vedic medicine. The species is native to western India, from where it was introduced westward to southern Pakistan and the middle-east. It prefers ari

india-34-haritaki.title: Terminalia chebula
india-34-haritaki.extract:
Terminalia chebula, commonly known as black- or chebulic myrobalan, is a species of Terminalia, native to South Asia from Pakistan,
India and Nepal east to southwest China (Yunnan), and south to Sri Lanka, Malaysia, and Vietnam.

india-35-bibhitaki.title: Terminalia bellirica
india-35-bibhitaki.extract: Terminalia bellirica, known as bahera, beleric or bastard myrobalan, is a large deciduous tree in the family Combretaceae. It is common on the plains and lower hills in South and Southeast Asia, where it is also grown as an avenue tree. The basionym is Myrobalanus bellirica Gaertn.. William Roxburgh transferred M. bellirica to Terminalia as "T. bellerica (Gaertn.) Roxb.". This spelling error is no

india-36-sandalwood.title: Santalum album
india-36-sandalwood.description: Species of tree in the sandalwood family
india-36-sandalwood.extract: Santalum album is a small tropical tree, and the traditional source of sandalwood oil. It is native to Indonesia, the Philippines, and Western Australia. It is commonly known as the white sandalwood or Indian sandalwood. It was one of the plants exploited by Austronesian arboriculture and it was introduced by Austronesian sailors to Mainland Southeast Asia, South Asia and East Asia, during the anc

india-37-vetiver.title: Chrysopogon zizanioides
india-37-vetiver.extract: Chrysopogon zizanioides, commonly known as vetiver and khus, is a perennial bunchgrass of the family Poaceae.

india-38-rose.title: Rosa × damascena
india-38-rose.description: Species of plant in the genus Rosa
india-38-rose.extract: Rosa × damascena, more commonly known as the Damask rose, or sometimes as the Iranian Rose, Bulgarian rose, Taif rose, Emirati rose, Ispahan rose, Isparta rose, and Castile rose, is a rose hybrid derived from Rosa gallica and Rosa moschata. DNA analysis has shown that a third species, Rosa fedtschenkoana, made some genetic contributions to the Damask rose.

india-39-saffron.title: Crocus sativus
india-39-saffron.extract: Crocus sativus, commonly known as saffron crocus or autumn crocus, is a species of flowering plant in the iris family Iridaceae. A cormous autumn-flowering cultivated perennial, unknown in the wild, it is best known for the culinary use of its floral stigmas as the spice saffron. Human cultivation of saffron crocus and the trade and use of saffron have endured for more than 3,500 years and span di

india-40-nutmeg.title: Myristica fragrans
india-40-nutmeg.description: Evergreen tree, source of nutmeg and mace
india-40-nutmeg.extract: Myristica fragrans, commonly known as the nutmeg tree, is an evergreen species indigenous to the Maluku Islands of Indonesia. This aromatic tree is economically significant as the primary source of two distinct spices: nutmeg, derived from its seed, and mace, obtained from the seed's aril.

india-41-fennel.title: Fennel
india-41-fennel.description: Flowering plant species in the carrot family
india-41-fennel.extract: Fennel is a flowering plant species in the carrot family. It is a hardy, perennial herb with yellow flowers and feathery leaves. It is indigenous to the shores of the Mediterranean but has become widely naturalized in many parts of the world, especially on dry soils near the sea coast and on riverbanks.

india-42-ajwain.title: Ajwain
india-42-ajwain.extract:
Ajwain or ajowan —also known as ajowan caraway, 
thymol seeds, bishop's weed, or carom—is an annual herb in the family Apiaceae. Both the leaves and the seed‑like fruit of the plant are consumed by humans. The name "bishop's weed" also is a common name for other plants. The "seed" is often confused with lovage seed.

india-43-asafoetida.title: Ferula narthex
india-43-asafoetida.extract: Ferula narthex is a species of plant native to Afghanistan, Tajikistan, northern Pakistan and Kashmir. Hugh Falconer noted that this was the source of asafoetida in Central Asia. Although it is often listed as the source of asafoetida, one report stated that its essential oil lacked sulfur-containing compounds which are characteristic of asafoetida.

india-44-kalmegh.title: Andrographis paniculata
india-44-kalmegh.description: Species of herb in the Acanthaceae family
india-44-kalmegh.extract: Andrographis paniculata, commonly known as creat or green chiretta, is an annual herbaceous plant in the family Acanthaceae, native to India and Sri Lanka.

india-45-karpooradi.title: Cinnamomum camphora
india-45-karpooradi.extract: Cinnamomum camphora, known as camphor tree or camphor laurel, is a species of evergreen tree that is commonly grown for camphor production. Karpooradi thailam is an Ayurvedic oil formulation with camphor as the main ingredient, used for pain relief and massage.

india-46-sesame-oil.title: Sesame
india-46-sesame-oil.description: Plant cultivated for its edible seeds
india-46-sesame-oil.extract: Sesame is a plant in the genus Sesamum, also called benne. Numerous wild relatives occur in Africa and a smaller number in India. It is widely naturalized in tropical regions around the world and is cultivated for its edible seeds, which grow in pods. World production in 2018 was 6 million tonnes, with Sudan, Myanmar, and India as the largest producers.

india-47-mustard-oil.title: Brassica juncea
india-47-mustard-oil.extract: Brassica juncea, commonly known as Indian mustard, brown mustard, or Chinese mustard, is a species of mustard plant. Mustard oil is extracted from the seeds of this plant and is widely used in Indian cuisine and traditional medicine.

india-48-chyawanprash-classical-rasayana.title: Chyavanprash
india-48-chyawanprash-classical-rasayana.extract: Chyavanprash is an ancient Ayurvedic herbal formulation first documented in the Charaka Samhita. It is named after the sage Chyavana Rishi, for whom it was prepared by the Ashvin twins. The formula contains amla (Indian gooseberry) as the primary ingredient along with numerous other herbs, honey, ghee, and spices.

india-49-sitopaladi-churna.title: Sitopaladi Churna
india-49-sitopaladi-churna.extract: Sitopaladi Churna is a classical Ayurvedic formulation beneficial for respiratory system, digestive system and immune system. It contains Misri (rock sugar), Tabasheer (bamboo silica), Piper Longum (long pepper), Elettaria Cardamomum (green cardamom) and Cinnamomum Zeylanicum (cinnamon). It is commonly used as a base for remedies beneficial in respiratory diseases.

india-50-hingvastak-churna.title: Hingvastak Churna
india-50-hingvastak-churna.extract: Hingvastak Churna is a classical Ayurvedic formulation used for digestive support. It contains Hing (asafoetida), Shunthi (ginger), Maricha (black pepper), Pippali (long pepper), Ajwain (ajwain), Saindhava lavana (rock salt), and other digestive herbs. It helps improve digestion, reduce bloating, and alleviate flatulence.

peru-1-coca-leaf.title: Erythroxylum coca
peru-1-coca-leaf.extract: Erythroxylum coca is a species of flowering plant in the family Erythroxylaceae. It is one of the two species of cultivated coca.

peru-2-maca.title: Lepidium meyenii
peru-2-maca.description: Plant species native to South America
peru-2-maca.extract: Lepidium meyenii, known as maca or Peruvian ginseng, is an edible herbaceous biennial plant of the family Brassicaceae that is native to South America in the high Andes mountains of Peru and Bolivia. It was rediscovered for commercial purposes at the Meseta de Bombón plateau close to Lake Junin in the late 1980s. It is grown for its fleshy hypocotyl that is fused with a taproot, which is typically

peru-3-cat-s-claw.title: Uncaria tomentosa
peru-3-cat-s-claw.extract: Uncaria tomentosa is a woody vine found in the tropical jungles of South and Central America. It is known as cat's claw or uña de gato in Spanish because of its claw-shaped thorns. The plant root bark is used in herbalism for a variety of ailments, and is sold as a dietary supplement. There is no high-quality clinical evidence that it has any benefit for treating human diseases.

peru-4-sangre-de-grado.title: Croton lechleri
peru-4-sangre-de-grado.extract: Croton lechleri is a species of flowering plant in the spurge family, Euphorbiaceae, that is native to northwestern South America. It is commonly known as sangre de grado, sangre de drago or sangre de grada. They refer to this tree's thick red latex.

peru-5-mu-a-andean-mint.title: Minthostachys mollis
peru-5-mu-a-andean-mint.extract: Minthostachys mollis is a medicinal plant restricted to the South American Andes from Peru to Bolivia. It is the most variable and widely distributed species of the genus Minthostachys. Its common name muña comes from Quechua. Other local names include tipo, tipollo, poleo.

peru-6-chuchuhuasi.title: Maytenus macrocarpa
peru-6-chuchuhuasi.extract: Maytenus macrocarpa, also known as Chuchuhuasi, is a large tree species native to the Amazon rainforest, growing in Bolivia, Colombia, Ecuador, Peru, Venezuela, and northern Brazil. It can reach heights of about 30 meters and is traditionally used in Amazonian medicine for its bark, which is prepared as a remedy for various ailments.

peru-7-copaiba-oil-spp.title: Copaifera
peru-7-copaiba-oil-spp.extract: Copaifera is a genus of tropical plants in the legume family Fabaceae. It includes 40 species native to the tropical Americas, west and central tropical Africa, and Borneo.

peru-8-aguaymanto.title: Physalis peruviana
peru-8-aguaymanto.description: Species of cultivated South American fruit
peru-8-aguaymanto.extract: Physalis peruviana is a species of plant in the nightshade family (Solanaceae) native to Chile and Peru. Within that region, it is called aguaymanto, uvilla or uchuva, in addition to numerous indigenous and regional names. In English, its common names include Cape gooseberry, goldenberry and Peruvian groundcherry.

peru-9-camu-camu.title: Myrciaria dubia
peru-9-camu-camu.description: Species of plant in the family Myrtaceae
peru-9-camu-camu.extract: Myrciaria dubia, commonly known as camu-camu, caçari, araçá-d'água, or camocamo, is a species of plant in the family Myrtaceae. It is a small bushy riverside tree from the Amazon rainforest in Peru and Brazil, which grows to a height of 3–5 metres and bears a red/purple cherry-like fruit. It is a close relative of the false jaboticaba and the guavaberry or rumberry. As much as 2 to 3% of the fresh

peru-10-sacha-inchi.title: Plukenetia volubilis
peru-10-sacha-inchi.extract: Plukenetia volubilis, commonly known as sacha inchi, sacha peanut, mountain peanut, Inca nut or Inca-peanut, is a perennial plant in the family Euphorbiaceae, having small trichomes on its leaves.

peru-11-hercampuri.title: Gentianella alborosea
peru-11-hercampuri.extract: Gentianella alborosea is a species of flowering plant in the family Gentianaceae. It is one of two types of hercampuri, which is traditionally used in herbal medicine as an infusion, the other type of hercampuri being Gentianella nitida. It has been used in Peruvian folk medicine since before the time of the Incas. These small shrubs are native to the high Andes of Peru. The Junin Province is a re

peru-12-chancapiedra.title: Phyllanthus niruri
peru-12-chancapiedra.extract: Phyllanthus niruri in the genus Phyllanthus of the family Phyllanthaceae is a widespread tropical plant commonly found in coastal areas from Texas southward through Mexico, Central America, and wide regions of South America and India. It has the common name chanca piedra among numerous others in Spanish.

peru-13-boldo.title: Boldo
peru-13-boldo.extract: Peumus boldus, commonly known as boldo, is a species of tree in the family Monimiaceae and the only species in the genus Peumus. It is endemic to the central region of Chile, between 33° and 40° southern latitude. Boldo has also been introduced to Europe and North Africa, though it is not often seen outside botanical gardens.

peru-14-hierba-luisa.title: Aloysia citrodora
peru-14-hierba-luisa.extract: Aloysia citrodora, lemon verbena, is a species of flowering plant in the verbena family Verbenaceae, native to South America. Other common names include lemon beebrush. It was brought to Europe by the Spanish and the Portuguese in the 17th century and cultivated for its oil.

peru-15-manayupa.title: Desmodium molliculum
peru-15-manayupa.extract: Desmodium molliculum, called manayupa or pata de perro in Peru, is a perennial herb in the pea family, Fabaceae.

peru-16-paico.title: Dysphania ambrosioides
peru-16-paico.extract: Dysphania ambrosioides, formerly Chenopodium ambrosioides, known as epazote, Jesuit's tea, Mexican tea or wormseed, is an annual or short-lived perennial herb native to the Americas.

peru-17-huamanripa.title: Senecio tephrosioides
peru-17-huamanripa.extract: Senecio tephrosioides, known as Huamanripa in Peru, is a species of plant in the Asteraceae family. It grows in the Andes Mountains and Amazon jungle. Traditionally used as a tea for chest and lung problems including coughs, bronchitis, and asthma.

peru-18-matico.title: Piper aduncum
peru-18-matico.extract: Piper aduncum, the spiked pepper, matico, hierba del soldado, achotlín, cordoncillo, higuillo or higuillo de hoja menuda, is a flowering plant in the family Piperaceae. Like many species in the family, the matico tree has a peppery odor. It grows wild on the coasts and in the forests of Central and South America and in the Interandean Valleys, up to 3,000 m (9,800 ft) above sea level.

peru-19-ayahuasca-psychotria-viridis.title: Banisteriopsis caapi
peru-19-ayahuasca-psychotria-viridis.extract: Banisteriopsis caapi, also known as, caapi, soul vine, yagé (yage), or ayahuasca is a South American liana of the family Malpighiaceae. It is commonly used as an ingredient of ayahuasca, a decoction with a long history of its entheogenic use and holds status as a "plant teacher" among the Indigenous peoples of the Amazon rainforest.

peru-20-san-pedro.title: Echinopsis pachanoi
peru-20-san-pedro.extract: Echinopsis pachanoi, synonyms including Trichocereus pachanoi and Trichocereus macrogonus var. pachanoi, is a fast-growing columnar cactus found in the Andes at 2,000–3,000 m (6,600–9,800 ft) in altitude. It is one of a number of kinds of cacti known as San Pedro cactus. It is native to Ecuador, and Peru, but is also found in Bolivia, Colombia, central Chile, the Canary Islands, and mainland Spain

peru-21-yerba-mate-south-american.title: Yerba mate
peru-21-yerba-mate-south-american.extract: Yerba mate or yerba maté, Ilex paraguariensis, is a plant species of the holly genus native to South America. It was named by the French botanist Augustin Saint-Hilaire. The leaves of the plant can be steeped in hot water to make a beverage known as mate. Brewed cold, it is used to make tereré. Both the plant and the beverage contain caffeine.

peru-22-achiote.title: Bixa orellana
peru-22-achiote.extract: Bixa orellana, also known as achiote, is a shrub or small tree native to Mexico and Central and South America. Bixa orellana is grown in many countries worldwide.

peru-23-graviola.title: Soursop
peru-23-graviola.extract: Soursop is the fruit of Annona muricata, a broadleaf, flowering, evergreen tree. It is native to the tropical Americas and is widely propagated. It is in the same genus, Annona, as cherimoya and is in the Annonaceae family.

peru-24-chirimoya.title: Cherimoya
peru-24-chirimoya.extract: The cherimoya, also spelled cherimoyer and chirimoya, and called chirimuya by the Quechua people, is a species of edible fruit-bearing plant in the genus Annona, within the family Annonaceae, which also includes the closely related sweetsop and soursop. Native to mountainous regions of Honduras and Guatemala, the plant has a long history of cultivation in the Andes and Central America.

peru-25-lucuma.title: Lucuma bifera
peru-25-lucuma.extract: Lucuma bifera is a species of tree in the family Sapotaceae, cultivated for its fruit, the lúcuma. It is native to the Andean valleys of Colombia, Ecuador, Peru, Bolivia, and northern Chile.

peru-26-kiwicha.title: Amaranthus caudatus
peru-26-kiwicha.extract: Amaranthus caudatus is a species of annual flowering plant. It goes by common names such as love-lies-bleeding, pendant amaranth, tassel flower, velvet flower, and foxtail amaranth.

peru-27-quinoa.title: Quinoa
peru-27-quinoa.description: Edible plant in the family Amaranthaceae
peru-27-quinoa.extract: Quinoa is a flowering plant in the amaranth family. It is an herbaceous annual plant grown as a crop primarily for its edible seeds; the seeds are high in protein, dietary fiber, B vitamins and dietary minerals especially potassium and magnesium in amounts greater than in many grains. Quinoa is not a grass but rather a pseudocereal botanically related to spinach and amaranth, and originated in the

peru-28-ca-ihua.title: Chenopodium pallidicaule
peru-28-ca-ihua.extract: Chenopodium pallidicaule, known as cañihua, canihua or cañahua and also kañiwa or kaniwa, is a species of goosefoot, similar in character and uses to the closely related quinoa.

peru-29-mashua.title: Tropaeolum tuberosum
peru-29-mashua.description: Species of flowering plant in the family Tropaeolaceae
peru-29-mashua.extract: Tropaeolum tuberosum is a species of flowering plant in the family Tropaeolaceae, grown in the Andes, particularly in Peru and Bolivia, and to a lesser extent in Ecuador as well as in some areas of Colombia, for its edible tubers, which are eaten cooked or roasted as a vegetable. It is a minor food source, especially for native Amerindian populations. Mashua is a herbaceous perennial climber growi

peru-30-maca-negra-black-maca.title: Lepidium meyenii
peru-30-maca-negra-black-maca.extract: Lepidium meyenii, known as maca or Peruvian ginseng, is an edible herbaceous biennial plant native to the high Andes mountains of Peru and Bolivia. Maca negra (black maca) is one of the varieties distinguished by its dark-colored root. It has been cultivated for over 2000 years and is used for its nutritional and adaptogenic properties.

peru-31-suma.title: Hebanthe erianthos
peru-31-suma.extract: Hebanthe erianthos, known as suma or Brazilian ginseng, is a species of plant in the family Amaranthaceae. The specific epithet is also spelt "eriantha", although the basionym is Iresine erianthos.

peru-32-jerg-n-sacha.title: Dracontium spruceanum
peru-32-jerg-n-sacha.extract: Dracontium spruceanum, known as Jergón Sacha in Peru, is a tropical succulent flowering plant species of the Amazon rainforest understory. It has been found in South American countries including Colombia, Ecuador, Peru, Suriname, and Venezuela, as well as in Central American countries like Costa Rica and Panama.

peru-33-ajo-sacha.title: Mansoa alliacea
peru-33-ajo-sacha.extract: Mansoa alliacea, or garlic vine, is a species of tropical liana in the family Bignoniaceae. It is native to Northern South America, and has spread to Central America and Brazil.

peru-34-chancapiedra-syrup-commercial.title: Phyllanthus niruri
peru-34-chancapiedra-syrup-commercial.extract: Phyllanthus niruri, known as chanca piedra in Spanish, is a widespread tropical plant commonly found in coastal areas from Texas southward through Mexico, Central America, and wide regions of South America and India. It grows 50-70 cm tall and bears ascending herbaceous branches with smooth light green bark and pale green flowers.

peru-35-algarrobina-carob-syrup.title: Neltuma pallida
peru-35-algarrobina-carob-syrup.extract: Neltuma pallida is a species of mesquite tree. It has the common names kiawe, huarango and American carob, as well as "bayahonda", "algarrobo pálido", and "algarrobo blanco". It is a thorny legume, native to Colombia, Ecuador and Peru, particularly drier areas near the coast. While threatened in its native habitat, it is considered an invasive species in many other places.

peru-36-u-a-de-gavil-n.title: Uncaria guianensis
peru-36-u-a-de-gavil-n.extract: Uncaria guianensis, the cat's claw, is a plant species in the genus Uncaria found in the Amazon biome. Other names for the plant include uña de gato, Paraguayo and vincaria.

peru-37-uchu-sanango.title: Brunfelsia grandiflora
peru-37-uchu-sanango.extract: Brunfelsia grandiflora is a flowering shrub in the nightshade family native to South America. In English it is known by the common names royal purple brunfelsia, kiss-me-quick, and yesterday-today-and-tomorrow. In Peru it is known by the Spanish-Quechua name chiricsanango or uchu sanango.

peru-38-manzanilla.title: Matricaria chamomilla
peru-38-manzanilla.extract: Matricaria chamomilla, commonly known as chamomile, German chamomile, Hungarian chamomile (kamilla), wild chamomile, blue chamomile, or scented mayweed, is an annual plant of the composite family Asteraceae. Commonly, the name M. recutita is applied to the herbal product chamomile, although other species are also used as chamomile. The plant is commonly used to make a tea.

peru-39-romero.title: Rosemary
peru-39-romero.extract: Salvia rosmarinus, synonym Rosmarinus officinalis, commonly known as rosemary, is a shrub with fragrant, evergreen, needle-like leaves and purple or sometimes white, pink, or blue flowers. It is a member of the mint family, Lamiaceae.

peru-40-tomillo.title: Thymus vulgaris
peru-40-tomillo.extract: Thymus vulgaris is a species of flowering plant in the mint family, Lamiaceae.

peru-41-salvia.title: Salvia officinalis
peru-41-salvia.extract: Salvia officinalis, common sage or sage, is a perennial, evergreen subshrub, with woody stems, grayish leaves, and blue to purplish flowers. It is a member of the mint family (Lamiaceae) and native to the Mediterranean region, though it has been naturalized in many places throughout the world. It has a long history of culinary use, and in modern times it has been used as an ornamental garden plant

peru-42-malva.title: Malva sylvestris
peru-42-malva.description: Plant species in the mallow family
peru-42-malva.extract: Malva sylvestris is a species of the mallow genus Malva, of which it the type species. Known as common mallow to English-speaking Europeans, it acquired the common names of cheeses, high mallow and tall mallow as it migrated from its native home in Western Europe, North Africa and Asia through the English-speaking world.

peru-43-cola-de-caballo.title: Equisetum arvense
peru-43-cola-de-caballo.extract: Equisetum arvense, commonly known as field horsetail or common horsetail, is a species of vascular plant in the horsetail family Equisetaceae. Like all horsetails, it is a type of fern. It is native throughout the arctic and temperate regions of the Northern Hemisphere. It has two distinct growth forms, a fertile spore-bearing stem and a sterile green stem. It is sometimes confused with mare's tai

peru-44-llant-n.title: Plantago major
peru-44-llant-n.description: Species of flowering plant in the plantain family Plantaginaceae
peru-44-llant-n.extract: Plantago major, the broadleaf plantain, white man's footprint, waybread, or greater plantain, is a species of flowering plant in the plantain family Plantaginaceae. The plant is native to Eurasia and naturalized to America. The leaves are edible and can be used to stop bleeding, soothe burns and insect bites.

peru-45-ortiga.title: Urtica dioica
peru-45-ortiga.description: Species of flowering plant in the family Urticaceae
peru-45-ortiga.extract: Urtica dioica, often known as common nettle, burn nettle, stinging nettle, nettle leaf, or just a nettle or stinger, is a herbaceous perennial flowering plant in the family Urticaceae. Originally native to Europe, much of temperate Asia and western North Africa, it is now found worldwide.

peru-46-diente-de-le-n.title: Taraxacum officinale
peru-46-diente-de-le-n.description: Flowering plant species known as dandelion
peru-46-diente-de-le-n.extract: Taraxacum officinale, the dandelion or common dandelion, is a herbaceous perennial flowering plant in the daisy family, Asteraceae. The common dandelion is well-known for its yellow flower heads that turn into round balls of many silver-tufted fruit, which disperse in the wind. These balls are sometimes called "clocks" or "blowballs".

peru-47-aloe.title: Aloe vera
peru-47-aloe.extract: Aloe vera is a succulent plant species of the genus Aloe. It is widely distributed, and is considered an invasive species in many world regions.

peru-48-or-gano-peruano.title: Lippia graveolens
peru-48-or-gano-peruano.extract: Lippia graveolens, a species of flowering plant in the verbena or vervain family, Verbenaceae, is native to the southwestern United States, Mexico, and Central America as far south as Nicaragua. Common names include: Mexican oregano, redbrush lippia, orégano cimarrón, scented lippia, and scented matgrass. The specific epithet is derived from two Latin words: gravis, meaning 'heavy', and olens, mea

peru-49-valeriana.title: Valerian (herb)
peru-49-valeriana.extract: Valerian is an herbaceous perennial flowering plant in the family Caprifoliaceae, native to Europe and southwestern Asia. It is the type species of the genus Valeriana.

peru-50-pasiflora.title: Passiflora incarnata
peru-50-pasiflora.extract: Passiflora incarnata, commonly known as maypop, purple passionflower, true passionflower, wild apricot, and wild passion vine, is a fast-growing perennial vine with climbing or trailing stems. A member of the passionflower genus Passiflora, the maypop has large, intricate flowers with prominent styles and stamens. One of the hardiest species of passionflower, it is both found as a wildflower in th

china-1-ginseng.title: Panax ginseng
china-1-ginseng.extract: Panax ginseng, ginseng, also known as Asian ginseng, Chinese ginseng or Korean ginseng, is a species of plant whose root is the original source of ginseng. It is a perennial plant that grows in the mountains of East Asia. It is mainly cultivated in China, Korea, Russia, and Japan.

china-2-astragalus.title: Astragalus mongholicus
china-2-astragalus.extract: Astragalus mongholicus, commonly known as Mongolian milkvetch in English; 'Хунчир' in Mongolian; huángqí, běiqí or huánghuā huángqí, in Mongolia, is a flowering plant in the family Fabaceae. It is one of the 50 fundamental herbs used in traditional Mongolian medicine. It is a perennial plant and it is not listed as being threatened.

china-3-goji.title: Lycium barbarum
china-3-goji.extract: Lycium barbarum is a shrub native to China, with present-day range across Asia and southeast Europe. It is one of two species of boxthorn in the family Solanaceae from which the goji berry or wolfberry is harvested, the other being Lycium chinense.

china-4-fresh-ginger.title: Zingiber officinale
china-4-fresh-ginger.description: Species of plant used as a spice
china-4-fresh-ginger.extract: Ginger is a flowering plant whose rhizome, ginger root or ginger, is widely used as a spice and a folk medicine. It is an herbaceous perennial that grows annual pseudostems about one meter tall, bearing narrow leaf blades. The inflorescences bear flowers having pale yellow petals with purple edges, and arise directly from the rhizome on separate shoots.

china-5-licorice.title: Glycyrrhiza uralensis
china-5-licorice.extract: Glycyrrhiza uralensis, also known as Chinese liquorice, is a flowering plant native to Asia. It is used as a sweetener and in traditional Chinese medicine.

china-6-white-peony.title: Paeonia lactiflora
china-6-white-peony.extract: Paeonia lactiflora is a species of herbaceous perennial flowering plant in the family Paeoniaceae, native to central and eastern Asia from eastern Tibet across northern China to eastern Siberia.

china-7-dang-gui.title: Angelica sinensis
china-7-dang-gui.extract: Angelica sinensis, commonly known as dong quai or female ginseng, is a herb belonging to the family Apiaceae, indigenous to China. A. sinensis grows in cool high altitude mountains in East Asia. The yellowish brown root of the plant is harvested in the fall and used in traditional Chinese medicine. There was previously no scientific evidence that A. sinensis is effective for any medicinal purpose.

china-8-reishi.title: Ganoderma lucidum
china-8-reishi.extract: Ganoderma lucidum, commonly known as the reishi, varnished conk, lacquered bracket, or lingzhi, is a red-colored species of Ganoderma with a limited distribution in Europe and parts of China, where it grows on decaying hardwood trees.

china-9-chrysanthemum.title: Chrysanthemum × morifolium
china-9-chrysanthemum.extract: Chrysanthemum × morifolium is a hybrid species of perennial plant in the genus Chrysanthemum of the family Asteraceae.

china-10-hawthorn.title: Crataegus pinnatifida
china-10-hawthorn.extract: Crataegus pinnatifida, also known as mountain hawthorn, Chinese haw, Chinese hawthorn or Chinese hawberry, refers to a small to medium-sized tree, as well as the fruit of the tree. The fruit is bright red, 1.5 in (38 mm) in diameter.

china-11-dang-shen.title: Codonopsis pilosula
china-11-dang-shen.extract: Codonopsis pilosula, also known as Dangshen, is a perennial species of flowering plant in the bellflower family. It is native to Asia, where it grows in forests, meadows, and scrub.

china-12-he-shou-wu-prepared-only.title: Pleuropterus multiflorus
china-12-he-shou-wu-prepared-only.extract: Pleuropterus multiflorus is a species of flowering plant in the buckwheat family Polygonaceae native to central and southern China, Hainan, Taiwan, Vietnam, and Thailand. It is known by the English common names tuber fleeceflower and Chinese (climbing) knotweed. It is known as he shou wu (何首烏) in China and East Asia. Another name for the species is fo-ti, which is a misnomer.

china-13-bai-zhu.title: Atractylodes macrocephala
china-13-bai-zhu.extract: Atractylodes macrocephala is a species of Atractylodes that grows in central China. The roots are consumed as a Chinese herbal medicine.

china-14-fu-ling.title: Wolfiporia extensa
china-14-fu-ling.extract: Wolfiporia extensa, commonly known as hoelen, poria, tuckahoe, China root, fu ling, or matsuhodo, is a species of fungus in the family Polyporaceae. It is a wood-decay fungus but has a subterranean growth habit. It notably develops a large, long-lasting underground sclerotium resembling a small coconut.

china-15-chen-pi-aged-tangerine-peel.title: Chenpi
china-15-chen-pi-aged-tangerine-peel.extract: Chenpi is sun-dried tangerine peel used as a traditional seasoning in Chinese cooking and traditional medicine. The practice of using citrus peels in traditional Chinese medicine originated in the Song dynasty. It contains volatile oils including nobiletin, hesperidin, and vitamin C.

china-16-ban-xia-processed.title: Pinellia ternata
china-16-ban-xia-processed.extract: Pinellia ternata, crow-dipper, is a plant that is native to China, Japan, and Korea. However, it also grows as an invasive weed in parts of Europe and in North America. The leaves are trifoliate, and the flowers are of the spathe and spadix form that is typical of plants in the family Araceae.

china-17-jin-yin-hua-honeysuckle.title: Lonicera japonica
china-17-jin-yin-hua-honeysuckle.description: Flowering vine known as Japanese honeysuckle
china-17-jin-yin-hua-honeysuckle.extract: Lonicera japonica, known as Japanese honeysuckle and golden-and-silver honeysuckle, is a species of honeysuckle native to East Asia, including many parts of China. It is often grown as an ornamental plant, but has become an invasive species in a number of countries. It is used in traditional Chinese medicine.

china-18-lian-qiao.title: Forsythia suspensa
china-18-lian-qiao.description: Species of flowering plant in the olive family Oleaceae
china-18-lian-qiao.extract: Forsythia suspensa, commonly known as weeping forsythia or golden-bell, is a species of flowering plant in the family Oleaceae. It is native to China.

china-19-ban-lan-gen.title: Isatis
china-19-ban-lan-gen.extract: Isatis is a genus of flowering plants in the family Brassicaceae, native to the Mediterranean region east to central Asia. Its genus name, Isatis, derives from the ancient Greek word for the plant, ἰσάτις. The genus includes woad. Due to their extremely variable morphology, the Asian species in particular are difficult to determine; the only reliable diagnostic feature is the ripe fruit. They are

china-20-huang-lian.title: Coptis chinensis
china-20-huang-lian.extract: Coptis chinensis, the Chinese goldthread, is a species of goldthread flowering plant native to China. It is one of the 50 fundamental herbs used in traditional Chinese medicine, where it is called duǎn è huánglián. The rhizomes contain the isoquinoline alkaloids berberine, palmatine, and coptisine.

china-21-huang-qin.title: Scutellaria baicalensis
china-21-huang-qin.extract: Scutellaria baicalensis, with the common name Baikal skullcap or Chinese skullcap, is a species of flowering plant in the family Lamiaceae.

china-22-huang-bai.title: Phellodendron amurense
china-22-huang-bai.extract: Phellodendron amurense is a species of tree in the family Rutaceae, commonly called the Amur cork tree. It is a major source of huáng bò, one of the 50 fundamental herbs used in traditional Chinese medicine. The Ainu people used its fruit, called "shikerebe-ni", as a painkiller. It is known as hwangbyeok in Korean and (キハダ) kihada in Japanese.

china-23-bo-he-chinese-mint.title: Mentha arvensis
china-23-bo-he-chinese-mint.extract: Mentha arvensis, known as corn mint, field mint, wild mint, or Chinese mint (Bo He), is a herbaceous perennial plant generally growing to 10-60 cm tall. In traditional Chinese medicine, the leaves are used to treat colds, aid digestion, and promote circulation. The variety haplocalyx is specifically known as Chinese mint.

china-24-ju-hong.title: Camellia sinensis
china-24-ju-hong.extract: Camellia sinensis is a species of evergreen shrub or small tree in the flowering plant family Theaceae. Its leaves, leaf buds, and stems are used to produce tea. Common names include tea plant, tea shrub, and tea tree.

china-25-dried-longan.title: Dimocarpus longan
china-25-dried-longan.extract: Dimocarpus longan, commonly known as longan, is a tropical tree species that produces edible fruit. It is native to southern China and Southeast Asia. The fruit is spherical, about 2.5 cm wide, with tan thin leathery shell and translucent sweet flesh. In Chinese, it's called 龙眼 meaning 'dragon eye' due to the black seed resembling an eyeball.

china-26-hong-zao.title: Jujube
china-26-hong-zao.description: Species of plant with edible fruit
china-26-hong-zao.extract: Jujube, sometimes jujuba, scientific name Ziziphus jujuba, and also called red date, Chinese date, and Chinese jujube, is a species in the genus Ziziphus in the buckthorn family Rhamnaceae. It is often confused with the closely related Indian jujube, Z. mauritiana. The jujube tolerates a diverse range of climates, from temperate to tropical. Its origin is thought to be in eastern Asia, but it has

china-27-suan-zao-ren-sour-jujube-seed.title: Ziziphus jujuba
china-27-suan-zao-ren-sour-jujube-seed.description: Species of plant with edible fruit
china-27-suan-zao-ren-sour-jujube-seed.extract: Ziziphus jujuba, known as jujube, red date, Chinese date, or sour jujube (Suan Zao Ren), is a small deciduous tree or shrub reaching 5-10 meters tall. The fruit is an edible oval drupe 1.5-3 cm deep, maturing from green to brown to purplish-black. The sour variety seeds are used in traditional Chinese medicine to calm the spirit and nourish the heart.

china-28-yuan-zhi.title: Polygala
china-28-yuan-zhi.extract: Polygala is a large genus of flowering plants belonging to the family Polygalaceae. They are commonly known as milkworts or snakeroots. The genus is distributed widely throughout much of the world in temperate zones and the tropics. The genus name Polygala comes from the ancient Greek "much milk", as the plant was thought to increase milk yields in cattle.

china-29-bai-he-lily-bulb.title: Lilium
china-29-bai-he-lily-bulb.extract: Lilium is a genus of herbaceous flowering plants growing from bulbs, all with large and often prominent flowers. Lilies are tall perennials ranging in height from 1-6 ft, forming naked or tunicless scaly underground bulbs. In Chinese medicine, lily bulbs (Bai He) are used to nourish yin, clear heat, and calm the spirit.

china-30-sha-shen.title: Glehnia
china-30-sha-shen.extract: Glehnia is a genus of plants in the carrot family, Apiaceae. It is monotypic, being represented by the single species, Glehnia littoralis, commonly known as beach silvertop and American silvertop. The genus was named after Baltic German botanist Peter von Glehn.

china-31-mai-men-dong.title: Ophiopogon
china-31-mai-men-dong.extract: Ophiopogon (lilyturf) is a genus of evergreen perennial plants native to warm temperate to tropical East, Southeast, and South Asia. Despite their grasslike appearance, they are not closely related to the true grasses, the Poaceae. The name of the genus is derived from Greek ὄφις ophis, 'snake' and πώγων pogon, 'beard', most probably referring to its leaves and tufted growth. In the APG III classi

china-32-shan-yao-chinese-yam.title: Dioscorea oppositifolia
china-32-shan-yao-chinese-yam.extract: Dioscorea oppositifolia is a type of yam (Dioscorea) native to Myanmar (Burma) and to the Indian subcontinent.

china-33-yi-yi-ren-job-s-tears.title: Coix lacryma-jobi
china-33-yi-yi-ren-job-s-tears.extract: Coix lacryma-jobi, known as Job's tears, adlay or adlay millet, is a tall grain-bearing perennial tropical plant of the grass family. The seeds are known in Chinese as yìyǐ rén and are used in traditional Chinese medicine for their diuretic and anti-inflammatory properties.

china-34-chi-xiao-dou-adzuki.title: Vigna angularis
china-34-chi-xiao-dou-adzuki.extract: Vigna angularis, also known as the adzuki bean, azuki bean, aduki bean, red bean, or red mung bean, is an annual vine widely cultivated throughout East Asia for its small bean. It is an important source of starch and protein and is used in traditional Chinese medicine as chi xiao dou.

china-35-lian-zi-lotus-seed.title: Nelumbo nucifera
china-35-lian-zi-lotus-seed.extract: Nelumbo nucifera, known as sacred lotus, is an aquatic perennial with large leaves and flowers. It has a very long history of being cultivated for its edible seeds. The seeds can remain dormant for an extensive period and are used in traditional Chinese medicine as lian zi.

china-36-bai-mu-er-white-fungus.title: Tremella fuciformis
china-36-bai-mu-er-white-fungus.extract: Tremella fuciformis is a species of fungus commonly known as snow fungus, snow ear, silver ear fungus, white jelly mushroom, and white cloud ears. It produces white, frond-like, gelatinous fruit bodies. In Chinese it is called bai mu'er.

china-37-hei-mu-er-black-wood-ear.title: Auricularia heimuer
china-37-hei-mu-er-black-wood-ear.extract: Auricularia heimuer, also known as heimuer or black wood ear, is a species of fungus in the order Auriculariales. It is commercially cultivated for food in China. Fruitbodies are gelatinous, ear-shaped, and laterally attached to wood, up to 12 centimetres across.

china-38-gui-zhi-cinnamon-twig.title: Cinnamomum cassia
china-38-gui-zhi-cinnamon-twig.extract: Cinnamomum cassia, called Chinese cassia, cassia cinnamon, or Chinese cinnamon, is an evergreen tree originating in southern China. The twigs are used in traditional Chinese medicine as gui zhi to warm the body and promote circulation.

china-39-rou-gui-cinnamon-bark.title: Cinnamomum cassia
china-39-rou-gui-cinnamon-bark.extract: Cinnamomum cassia, called Chinese cassia, cassia cinnamon, or Chinese cinnamon, is an evergreen tree originating in southern China and widely cultivated there and elsewhere in South and Southeast Asia. It is one of several species of Cinnamomum used primarily for its aromatic bark, which is used as a spice.

china-40-da-huang-rhubarb-root.title: Rheum palmatum
china-40-da-huang-rhubarb-root.extract: Rheum palmatum, known as Chinese rhubarb or da huang, is a perennial herbaceous plant growing to 1.5–2 m tall. Its lobed leaves are large, jagged and hand-shaped. Chinese rhubarb has thick, deep roots used in traditional Chinese medicine.

china-41-ma-huang.title: Ephedra sinica
china-41-ma-huang.extract: Ephedra sinica, known in Chinese as ma huang, is a plant used in traditional Chinese medicine. It is a dioecious evergreen shrub, a gymnosperm closely related to conifers, with small leaves on green stems. The stems and leaves are collected and dried for use.

china-42-xing-ren-bitter-apricot-kernel.title: Prunus armeniaca
china-42-xing-ren-bitter-apricot-kernel.extract: Prunus armeniaca is a small tree, 8–12 m tall, with a dense, spreading canopy. The fruit is a drupe similar to a small peach, from yellow to orange. The single seed is enclosed in a hard, stony shell. In traditional Chinese medicine, the bitter apricot kernels are used as xing ren for respiratory conditions.

china-43-pi-pa-ye-loquat-leaf.title: Eriobotrya japonica
china-43-pi-pa-ye-loquat-leaf.extract: Eriobotrya japonica is a large evergreen shrub or small tree, known as loquat. The leaves are alternate, simple, 10–25 centimetres long, dark green, tough and leathery in texture, with a serrated margin. In traditional Chinese medicine, the leaves are used as pi pa ye for respiratory conditions.

china-44-san-qi.title: Panax notoginseng
china-44-san-qi.extract: Panax notoginseng is a species of the genus Panax, and it is commonly referred to in English as Chinese ginseng or notoginseng. In Chinese it is called tiánqī, tienchi ginseng, sānqī or sanchi, three-seven root, and mountain plant. P. notoginseng belongs to the same scientific genus as Panax ginseng. In Latin, the word panax means "cure-all", and the family of ginseng plants is one of the best-kno

china-45-du-zhong.title: Eucommia ulmoides
china-45-du-zhong.extract: Eucommia ulmoides is a species of small tree native to China. It belongs to the monotypic family Eucommiaceae. It is considered vulnerable in the wild, but is widely cultivated in China for its bark and is highly valued in herbology such as traditional Chinese medicine.

china-46-nu-zhen-zi.title: Ligustrum lucidum
china-46-nu-zhen-zi.extract: Ligustrum lucidum, the broad-leaf privet, Chinese privet, glossy privet, tree privet or wax-leaf privet, is a species of flowering plant in the olive family Oleaceae, native to the southern half of China and naturalized in many places. The name "Chinese privet" is also used for Ligustrum sinense.

china-47-gou-teng.title: Uncaria rhynchophylla
china-47-gou-teng.extract: Uncaria rhynchophylla or the cat's claw herb is a plant species used in traditional Chinese medicine.

china-48-tian-ma.title: Gastrodia elata
china-48-tian-ma.extract: Gastrodia elata is a mycoheterotrophic perennial herb in the family Orchidaceae. It is found in Nepal, Bhutan, India, Japan, Korea, Siberia, Taiwan, and China.

china-49-cordyceps.title: Ophiocordyceps sinensis
china-49-cordyceps.extract: Ophiocordyceps sinensis (synonym Cordyceps sinensis), known colloquially as caterpillar fungus, is an entomopathogenic fungus in the family Ophiocordycipitaceae. It is mainly found in the meadows above 3,500 metres on the Tibetan Plateau regions of Tibet, Qinghai, Sichuan, Yunnan and Himalayan regions of Bhutan and Nepal.

china-50-yunnan-baiyao-proprietary-powde.title: Yunnan Baiyao
china-50-yunnan-baiyao-proprietary-powde.extract: Yunnan Baiyao, meaning 'White medicine from Yunnan', is a traditional Chinese medicine developed by Qu Huanzhang in 1902. It is a hemostatic powder formulation used to stop bleeding and promote wound healing. It gained fame during the Battle of Taierzhuang in 1938 and is considered a valuable traditional remedy in China.

japan-1-yomogi-japanese-mugwort.title: Artemisia princeps
japan-1-yomogi-japanese-mugwort.extract: Artemisia princeps, also commonly called yomogi, ssuk, Japanese mugwort, Korean wormwood, Korean mugwort or wormwood in English, is an Asian plant species in the sunflower family, native to China, Japan and Korea. It is a perennial, very vigorous plant that grows to 1.2 meters. This species spreads rapidly by means of underground stolons and can become invasive. It bears small, buff-colored flower

japan-2-shiso.title: Perilla frutescens
japan-2-shiso.extract: Perilla frutescens, known as deulkkae or Korean perilla in Korea, as egoma in Japan, and as zisu in China, is a species of Perilla in the mint family Lamiaceae. It is an annual plant native to Southeast Asia and Indian highlands, and is traditionally grown in the Korean peninsula, southern China, Japan and India as a crop. A variety of this plant, P. frutescens var. crispa known as "shiso", is wid

japan-3-dokudami.title: Houttuynia cordata
japan-3-dokudami.extract: Houttuynia cordata, also known as fish mint, fish leaf, rainbow plant, chameleon plant, heart leaf, fish wort, or Chinese lizard tail, is one of two species in the genus Houttuynia. It is a flowering plant native to Southeast Asia. It grows in moist, shady locations. It was named after Martinus Houttuyn.

japan-4-biwa-ha-loquat-leaf.title: Eriobotrya japonica
japan-4-biwa-ha-loquat-leaf.extract: Eriobotrya japonica, known as loquat, is a large evergreen shrub or small tree in the family Rosaceae, native to the cooler hill regions of south-central China. The leaves are alternate, simple, 10-25 cm long, dark green, tough and leathery with serrated margins. In Japanese medicine, loquat leaves (Biwa Ha) are used for their soothing and expectorant properties.

japan-5-ryokucha-green-tea.title: Camellia sinensis
japan-5-ryokucha-green-tea.extract: Camellia sinensis is a species of evergreen shrub or small tree in the flowering plant family Theaceae. Its leaves, leaf buds, and stems are used to produce tea. Common names include tea plant, tea shrub, and tea tree.

japan-6-sh-ga-ginger.title: Zingiber officinale
japan-6-sh-ga-ginger.description: Species of plant used as a spice
japan-6-sh-ga-ginger.extract: Ginger is a flowering plant whose rhizome, ginger root or ginger, is widely used as a spice and a folk medicine. It is an herbaceous perennial that grows annual pseudostems about one meter tall, bearing narrow leaf blades. In Japanese medicine, fresh ginger (Sho Ga) is used to warm the body and aid digestion.

japan-7-umeboshi-pickled-plum.title: Prunus mume
japan-7-umeboshi-pickled-plum.extract: Prunus mume, known as ume or Japanese apricot, is a species of fruit-bearing tree in the genus Prunus. Umeboshi are pickled (brined) ume fruits common in Japan. The word umeboshi is often translated as 'salted Japanese plums' or 'preserved plums'. They are traditionally made by harvesting ume fruit and packing them in barrels with salt for about two weeks.

japan-8-yomogi-mochi.title: Artemisia princeps
japan-8-yomogi-mochi.extract: Artemisia princeps, known as Japanese mugwort or yomogi, is native to China, Japan and Korea. It grows in a variety of habitats including roadsides, slopes, valleys, and riverbanks. The leaves and young seedlings can be eaten raw or cooked, and are traditionally used in Japanese cuisine to make yomogi mochi (mugwort rice cakes).

japan-9-kuromoji.title: Litsea cubeba
japan-9-kuromoji.extract: Litsea cubeba, known as aromatic litsea, may chang, or kuromoji in Japanese, is an evergreen tree or shrub 5-12 meters high in the Lauraceae family. It is native to China, Japan, Taiwan and Southeast Asia. The essential oil from the fruit contains 70-85% citral and is used as a spice and fragrance.

japan-10-mitsuba.title: Cryptotaenia japonica
japan-10-mitsuba.extract: Cryptotaenia japonica, commonly called mitsuba, Japanese wild parsley and Japanese honewort among other names, is a herbaceous perennial plant in the celery family native to Japan, Korea and China. The plant is edible and is commonly used as a garnish and root vegetable in Japan, and other Asian countries. It is sometimes considered a subspecies of Cryptotaenia canadensis as Cryptotaenia canadensi

japan-11-kuzu-root-starch.title: Pueraria montana var. lobata
japan-11-kuzu-root-starch.extract: Pueraria montana var. lobata, the East Asian arrowroot, or kudzu vine, is a perennial plant in the family Fabaceae.

japan-12-kakkon-t-classical-formula.title: Kakkon-to
japan-12-kakkon-t-classical-formula.extract: Kakkon-to is a classical Japanese Kampo formula containing Pueraria root (Kakkon), Ephedra herb (Mao), Cinnamon twig (Keihi), Paeonia root (Shakuyaku), Jujube (Taiso), Glycyrrhiza (Kanzo), and Ginger (Shokyo). It is traditionally used for colds, flu, and early-stage fevers with chills.

japan-13-sh-saikot.title: Sho-saiko-to
japan-13-sh-saikot.extract: Sho-saiko-to is a classical Japanese Kampo formula containing Bupleurum root (Saiko), Pinellia tuber (Hange), Scutellaria root (Ogon), Ginger (Shokyo), Jujube (Taiso), Ginseng (Ninjin), Glycyrrhiza (Kanzo), and Peony root (Shakuyaku). It is traditionally used for liver support, digestive issues, and stress-related conditions.

japan-14-hochu-ekki-t.title: Hochu-ekki-to
japan-14-hochu-ekki-t.extract: Hochu-ekki-to is a classical Japanese Kampo formula containing Astragalus root (Ougi), Atractylodes rhizome (Byakujutsu), Ginseng (Ninjin), Angelica root (Toki), Citrus peel (Chinpi), Glycyrrhiza (Kanzo), Cimicifuga rhizome (Shoma), and Ginger (Shokyo). It is traditionally used to strengthen qi, improve digestion, and support recovery from illness.

japan-15-kakkon-t--ka-senky--shin-i.title: Kakkon-to-ka-senkyu-shin'i
japan-15-kakkon-t--ka-senky--shin-i.extract: Kakkon-to-ka-senkyu-shin'i is a classical Japanese Kampo formula based on Kakkon-to with the addition of Senkyu (Cnidium rhizome) and Shin'i (Magnolia flower). It is traditionally used for colds, nasal congestion, and headaches with accompanying fever and chills.

japan-16-bakumond--t.title: Bakumondo-to
japan-16-bakumond--t.extract: Bakumondo-to is a classical Japanese Kampo formula containing Ophiopogon tuber (Bakumondo), Pinellia tuber (Hange), Scutellaria root (Ogon), Licorice (Kanzo), Jujube (Taiso), and Ginger (Shokyo). It is traditionally used for dry cough, throat irritation, and respiratory issues.

japan-17-daikench-t.title: Daikenchu-to
japan-17-daikench-t.extract: Daikenchu-to is a classical Japanese Kampo formula containing Ginger (Shokyo), Ginseng (Ninjin), and Zanthoxylum fruit (Sansho). It is traditionally used for digestive support, to warm the body, and to improve gastrointestinal motility.

japan-18-t-ki-shakuyaku-san.title: Toki-shakuyaku-san
japan-18-t-ki-shakuyaku-san.extract: Toki-shakuyaku-san is a classical Japanese Kampo formula containing Angelica root (Toki), Peony root (Shakuyaku), Atractylodes rhizome (Byakujutsu), Alisma rhizome (Takusha), Poria (Bukuryo), and Cnidium rhizome (Senkyu). It is traditionally used for women's health, menstrual support, and blood circulation.

japan-19-kamishoy-san.title: Kamisho-san
japan-19-kamishoy-san.extract: Kamisho-san is a classical Japanese Kampo formula containing Bupleurum root (Saiko), Scutellaria root (Ogon), Pinellia tuber (Hange), Ginger (Shokyo), Jujube (Taiso), Licorice (Kanzo), Peony root (Shakuyaku), and Atractylodes rhizome (Byakujutsu). It is traditionally used for digestive issues, stress, and autonomic nervous system balance.

japan-20-yokukansan.title: Yokukansan
japan-20-yokukansan.extract: Yokukansan is a classical Japanese Kampo formula containing Atractylodes rhizome (Byakujutsu), Poria (Bukuryo), Cnidium rhizome (Senkyu), Uncaria stem (Chotoko), Japanese Angelica (Toki), Bupleurum root (Saiko), and Glycyrrhiza (Kanzo). It is traditionally used for irritability, insomnia, and behavioral issues.

japan-21-rikkunshit.title: Rikkunshito
japan-21-rikkunshit.extract: Rikkunshito is a classical Japanese Kampo formula containing Atractylodes rhizome (Byakujutsu), Ginseng (Ninjin), Pinellia tuber (Hange), Poria (Bukuryo), Jujube (Taiso), Licorice (Kanzo), Citrus peel (Chinpi), Ginger (Shokyo), and Zanthoxylum fruit (Sansho). It is traditionally used for digestive support, appetite stimulation, and gastrointestinal motility.

japan-22-hangeshashint.title: Hangeshashin-to
japan-22-hangeshashint.extract: Hangeshashin-to is a classical Japanese Kampo formula containing Pinellia tuber (Hange), Scutellaria root (Ogon), Glycyrrhiza (Kanzo), Ginger (Shokyo), Jujube (Taiso), and Ginseng (Ninjin). It is traditionally used for gastrointestinal issues, nausea, and to support digestion during illness recovery.

japan-23-goshajinkigan.title: Goshajinkigan
japan-23-goshajinkigan.extract: Goshajinkigan is a classical Japanese Kampo formula containing Rehmannia root (Jio), Cornus fruit (Sanzashi), Dioscorea rhizome (Sannyaku), Poria (Bukuryo), Alisma rhizome (Takusha), Cinnamon bark (Keihi), and Aconite root (Bushi). It is traditionally used for kidney support, lower back pain, and urinary issues.

japan-24-mugi-cha-roasted-barley-tea.title: Hordeum vulgare
japan-24-mugi-cha-roasted-barley-tea.description: Species of plant used as a cereal
japan-24-mugi-cha-roasted-barley-tea.extract: Hordeum vulgare, known as barley, is a member of the grass family. Mugi-cha is roasted barley tea made from roasted barley grains. It is a caffeine-free beverage popular in Japan and Korea, with a mild, nutty flavor and digestive benefits.

japan-25-sobacha-buckwheat-tea.title: Fagopyrum esculentum
japan-25-sobacha-buckwheat-tea.description: Species of plant used as a cereal
japan-25-sobacha-buckwheat-tea.extract: Fagopyrum esculentum, known as buckwheat, is a plant cultivated for its grain-like seeds. Sobacha is roasted buckwheat tea made from roasted buckwheat groats. It is a caffeine-free beverage popular in Japan, with a nutty flavor and rich in rutin.

japan-26-genmaicha-brown-rice-green-tea.title: Camellia sinensis
japan-26-genmaicha-brown-rice-green-tea.extract: Camellia sinensis is a species of evergreen shrub or small tree in the flowering plant family Theaceae. Its leaves, leaf buds, and stems are used to produce tea. Genmaicha is a Japanese green tea combined with roasted brown rice, creating a nutty flavor and lower caffeine content.

japan-27-h-jicha-roasted-green-tea.title: Camellia sinensis
japan-27-h-jicha-roasted-green-tea.extract: Camellia sinensis is a species of evergreen shrub or small tree in the flowering plant family Theaceae. Its leaves, leaf buds, and stems are used to produce tea. Hojicha is a Japanese roasted green tea with a reddish-brown color and lower caffeine content, with a toasty, caramel-like flavor.

japan-28-matcha.title: Camellia sinensis
japan-28-matcha.extract: Camellia sinensis is a species of evergreen shrub or small tree in the flowering plant family Theaceae. Matcha is a Japanese powdered green tea made from shade-grown tea leaves, ground into a fine powder. It is rich in antioxidants and used in traditional Japanese tea ceremonies.

japan-29-goma-sesame.title: Sesamum indicum
japan-29-goma-sesame.extract: Sesamum indicum, known as sesame, is a flowering plant in the genus Sesamum. Numerous wild relatives occur in Africa and a smaller number in India. It is widely naturalized in tropical regions around the world and is cultivated for its edible seeds.

japan-30-natto.title: Glycine max
japan-30-natto.extract: Glycine max, known as soybean, is a species of legume native to East Asia. Natto is a traditional Japanese food made from fermented soybeans. It is rich in protein, vitamin K2, and probiotics, and has a distinctive sticky texture and strong aroma.

japan-31-miso.title: Glycine max
japan-31-miso.extract: Glycine max, known as soybean, is a species of legume native to East Asia. Miso is a traditional Japanese seasoning produced by fermenting soybeans with salt and koji (the fungus Aspergillus oryzae). It is rich in protein, vitamins, and probiotics.

japan-32-shoyu-k-ji.title: Glycine max
japan-32-shoyu-k-ji.extract: Glycine max, known as soybean, is a species of legume native to East Asia. Shoyu (soy sauce) is a traditional Japanese condiment produced by fermenting soybeans with wheat, salt, and koji. It is rich in amino acids and used as a seasoning in Japanese cuisine.

japan-33-konbu-kelp.title: Saccharina japonica
japan-33-konbu-kelp.extract: Saccharina japonica, known as konbu or kombu, is a species of edible kelp widely consumed in East Asia. It is a key ingredient in Japanese dashi broth and is rich in iodine, minerals, and umami flavor compounds.

japan-34-wakame.title: Undaria pinnatifida
japan-34-wakame.extract: Undaria pinnatifida, known as wakame, is a species of edible seaweed native to cold temperate coastal areas of Japan, Korea, and China. It is widely used in Japanese cuisine, particularly in miso soup and salads, and is rich in minerals, vitamins, and omega-3 fatty acids.

japan-35-hijiki.title: Sargassum fusiforme
japan-35-hijiki.extract: Sargassum fusiforme, known as hijiki, is a brown seaweed widely consumed in Japan. It grows on rocky coastlines and is traditionally used in Japanese cuisine for its rich mineral content, particularly calcium, magnesium, and iron.

japan-36-ume-extract.title: Prunus mume
japan-36-ume-extract.extract: Prunus mume, known as ume or Japanese apricot, is a species of fruit-bearing tree in the genus Prunus. Ume extract is made from the fruit and is traditionally used in Japanese medicine for digestive support and as a natural preservative.

japan-37-ninniku-garlic-japanese-folk.title: Allium sativum
japan-37-ninniku-garlic-japanese-folk.description: Species of plant used as a spice
japan-37-ninniku-garlic-japanese-folk.extract: Allium sativum, known as garlic, is a species of bulbous flowering plant in the genus Allium. It is related to onion, shallot, leek, chive, and Chinese onion. In Japanese folk medicine, garlic (Ninniku) is used for immune support, cardiovascular health, and as a natural antimicrobial.

japan-38-aojiru-green-juice-kale.title: Brassica oleracea
japan-38-aojiru-green-juice-kale.extract: Brassica oleracea, known as kale, is a species of cabbage in the family Brassicaceae. Aojiru is a Japanese green juice typically made from kale or other leafy greens. It is rich in vitamins, minerals, and antioxidants, and is consumed for its health benefits.

japan-39-amazake.title: Oryza sativa
japan-39-amazake.description: Species of plant used as a cereal
japan-39-amazake.extract: Oryza sativa, known as Asian rice, is the plant species most commonly referred to as rice. Amazake is a traditional Japanese sweet, low-alcohol beverage made from fermented rice. It is rich in enzymes, vitamins, and beneficial bacteria, and is used as a natural sweetener and health tonic.

japan-40-yuzu.title: Yuzu
japan-40-yuzu.extract: Yuzu is a citrus fruit and plant originating in East Asia. It is believed to be a hybrid of ichang papeda and mandarin orange. The fruit resembles a yellow clementine with uneven skin and can be either yellow or green depending on the degree of ripeness. Yuzu fruits are very aromatic.

japan-41-sansh-japanese-pepper.title: Zanthoxylum piperitum
japan-41-sansh-japanese-pepper.extract: Zanthoxylum piperitum, also known as Japanese pepper or Japanese prickly-ash, is a deciduous aromatic spiny shrub or small tree of the citrus and rue family Rutaceae, native to Japan and Korea. It is called sanshō in Japan and chopi in Korea.

japan-42-wasabi.title: Eutrema japonicum
japan-42-wasabi.extract: Eutrema japonicum, known as wasabi or Japanese horseradish, is a plant of the family Brassicaceae, which also includes horseradish and mustard. It is native to Japan, Korea, and the Russian Far East. Its root is used as a strong condiment with a hot, pungent flavor.

japan-43-kanten-agar.title: Gelidium amansii
japan-43-kanten-agar.extract: Gelidium amansii is a species of red algae used to produce agar, known as kanten in Japanese. Agar is a gelatinous substance derived from seaweed, used as a vegetarian gelling agent and in traditional medicine for digestive support.

japan-44-gob-burdock-root.title: Arctium lappa
japan-44-gob-burdock-root.extract: Arctium lappa, known as greater burdock or gobō in Japanese, is a biennial plant of the family Asteraceae. It is cultivated for its large, edible root which is used in Japanese cuisine. The root is rich in inulin, fiber, and antioxidants.

japan-45-renkon-lotus-root.title: Nelumbo nucifera
japan-45-renkon-lotus-root.extract: Nelumbo nucifera, known as lotus, is a species of aquatic plant in the family Nelumbonaceae. Renkon is the edible rhizome (root) of the lotus plant, widely used in Japanese cuisine. It is rich in fiber, vitamins, and minerals, with a crisp texture.

japan-41-san-shishi.title: Panax ginseng
japan-41-san-shishi.extract: Panax ginseng, known as Asian ginseng, is a slow-growing perennial plant with fleshy roots. In traditional Chinese and Japanese medicine, ginseng is used as an adaptogen to help the body resist stress and improve overall vitality, energy, and cognitive function.

japan-47-kinkan-kumquat.title: Fortunella
japan-47-kinkan-kumquat.extract: Fortunella, known as kumquat, is a group of small fruit-bearing trees in the flowering plant family Rutaceae. Kinkan is the Japanese name for kumquat, which is eaten whole including the sweet rind. It is rich in vitamin C and antioxidants.

japan-46-daikon.title: Raphanus sativus
japan-46-daikon.extract: Raphanus sativus, known as daikon or Japanese radish, is a root vegetable species in the family Brassicaceae. It is a large, white radish widely used in Japanese cuisine for its crisp texture and mild flavor. It is rich in vitamin C, enzymes, and fiber.

japan-48-karin-chinese-quince.title: Pseudocydonia sinensis
japan-48-karin-chinese-quince.extract: Pseudocydonia sinensis, known as Chinese quince or karin in Japanese, is a flowering tree in the family Rosaceae. The fruit is hard and astringent when raw, but becomes sweet and fragrant when cooked. It is used in traditional medicine for respiratory support.

japan-49-kikurage-wood-ear.title: Auricularia auricula-judae
japan-49-kikurage-wood-ear.extract: Auricularia auricula-judae, known as wood ear or kikurage in Japanese, is a species of edible jelly fungus. It grows on trees and is widely used in Asian cuisine for its crunchy texture. It is rich in iron, fiber, and antioxidants.

japan-50-akamoku-seaweed.title: Sargassum horneri
japan-50-akamoku-seaweed.extract: Sargassum horneri, known as akamoku in Japanese, is a species of brown seaweed found in the coastal waters of East Asia. It is edible and used in Japanese cuisine, rich in minerals, vitamins, and dietary fiber.

global-1-echinacea-native-american.title: Echinacea purpurea
global-1-echinacea-native-american.description: Species of flowering plant in the daisy family
global-1-echinacea-native-american.extract: Echinacea purpurea, the eastern purple coneflower, purple coneflower, hedgehog coneflower, or Echinacea, is a North American species of flowering plant in the family Asteraceae. It is native to parts of eastern North America and present to some extent in the wild in much of the eastern, southeastern and midwestern United States, as well as in the Canadian Province of Ontario. It is most common in

global-2-elderberry.title: Sambucus nigra
global-2-elderberry.description: Species of flowering plant in the moschatel family
global-2-elderberry.extract: Sambucus nigra is a species complex of flowering plants in the family Viburnaceae native to most of Europe. Common names include elder, elderberry, black elder, European elder,  European elderberry, and European black elderberry. It grows in a variety of conditions including both wet and dry fertile soils, primarily in sunny locations. The plant is widely grown as an ornamental shrub or small tree

global-3-garlic-mediterranean.title: Garlic
global-3-garlic-mediterranean.extract: Garlic is a species of bulbous flowering plants in the genus Allium. Its close relatives include the onion, shallot, leek, chives, Welsh onion, and Chinese onion. Garlic is native to central and western Asia, stretching from the Black Sea through the southern Caucasus, northeastern Iran, and the Hindu Kush. It has naturalized in many other parts of the world, including Mediterranean Europe and Chi

global-4-oregano.title: Oregano
global-4-oregano.extract: Oregano is a species of flowering plant in the mint family, Lamiaceae. It was native to the Mediterranean region, but widely naturalised elsewhere in the temperate Northern Hemisphere.

global-5-thyme.title: Thymus vulgaris
global-5-thyme.extract: Thymus vulgaris is a species of flowering plant in the mint family, Lamiaceae.

global-6-rosemary.title: Rosemary
global-6-rosemary.extract: Salvia rosmarinus, synonym Rosmarinus officinalis, commonly known as rosemary, is a shrub with fragrant, evergreen, needle-like leaves and purple or sometimes white, pink, or blue flowers. It is a member of the mint family, Lamiaceae.

global-7-sage.title: Salvia officinalis
global-7-sage.extract: Salvia officinalis, common sage or sage, is a perennial, evergreen subshrub, with woody stems, grayish leaves, and blue to purplish flowers. It is a member of the mint family (Lamiaceae) and native to the Mediterranean region, though it has been naturalized in many places throughout the world. It has a long history of culinary use, and in modern times it has been used as an ornamental garden plant

global-8-lavender.title: Lavandula angustifolia
global-8-lavender.extract: Lavandula angustifolia, formerly L. officinalis, is a flowering plant in the family Lamiaceae, native to the Mediterranean basin. Its common names include lavender, true lavender and English lavender ; also garden lavender, common lavender and narrow-leaved lavender.

global-9-chamomile.title: Matricaria chamomilla
global-9-chamomile.extract: Matricaria chamomilla, commonly known as chamomile, German chamomile, Hungarian chamomile (kamilla), wild chamomile, blue chamomile, or scented mayweed, is an annual plant of the composite family Asteraceae. Commonly, the name M. recutita is applied to the herbal product chamomile, although other species are also used as chamomile. The plant is commonly used to make a tea.

global-10-peppermint.title: Peppermint
global-10-peppermint.description: Hybrid flowering plant in the family Lamiaceae
global-10-peppermint.extract: Peppermint is a hybrid species of mint, a cross between watermint and spearmint. Indigenous to Europe and the Middle East, the plant is now widely spread and cultivated in many regions of the world. It is occasionally found in the wild with its parent species.

global-11-fennel.title: Fennel
global-11-fennel.description: Flowering plant species in the carrot family
global-11-fennel.extract: Fennel is a flowering plant species in the carrot family. It is a hardy, perennial herb with yellow flowers and feathery leaves. It is indigenous to the shores of the Mediterranean but has become widely naturalized in many parts of the world, especially on dry soils near the sea coast and on riverbanks.

global-12-calendula.title: Calendula officinalis
global-12-calendula.description: Species of flowering plant in the daisy family Asteraceae
global-12-calendula.extract: Calendula officinalis, Mary's gold, common marigold, the pot marigold, Scotch marigold, or ruddles, is a flowering plant in the daisy family, Asteraceae.
