It is probably native to southern Europe, but its long history of cultivation makes its precise origin unknown, and it is widely naturalised. The florets are edible and the plant has historically been used as medicine.

global-13-st-john-s-wort.title: Hypericum perforatum
global-13-st-john-s-wort.description: Flowering plant in the St John's wort family
global-13-st-john-s-wort.extract: Hypericum perforatum, commonly known as St. John's wort, is a flowering plant in the family Hypericaceae. It is a hairless, perennial herb with woody roots, yellow flowers marked by black glands, and leaves that appear perforated due to translucent glands, producing thousands of seeds per plant.

global-14-valerian.title: Valerian (herb)
global-14-valerian.extract: Valerian is an herbaceous perennial flowering plant in the family Caprifoliaceae, native to Europe and southwestern Asia. It is the type species of the genus Valeriana.

global-15-hops.title: Humulus lupulus
global-15-hops.extract: Humulus lupulus, the common hop or hops, is a species of flowering plant in the hemp family, Cannabaceae. It is a perennial, herbaceous climbing plant which sends up new shoots in early spring and dies back to a cold-hardy rhizome in autumn. It is dioecious and native to West Asia, Europe and North America.

global-16-milk-thistle.title: Silybum marianum
global-16-milk-thistle.description: Species of plant of genus Silybum in family Asteraceae
global-16-milk-thistle.extract: Silybum marianum is a species of thistle. It has various common names including milk thistle, blessed milkthistle, Marian thistle, Mary thistle, Saint Mary's thistle, Mediterranean milk thistle, variegated thistle and Scotch thistle. This species is an annual or biennial plant of the family Asteraceae. This fairly typical thistle has red to purple flowers and shiny pale green leaves with white vei

global-17-dandelion.title: Taraxacum officinale
global-17-dandelion.description: Flowering plant species known as dandelion
global-17-dandelion.extract: Taraxacum officinale, the dandelion or common dandelion, is a herbaceous perennial flowering plant in the daisy family, Asteraceae. The common dandelion is well-known for its yellow flower heads that turn into round balls of many silver-tufted fruit, which disperse in the wind. These balls are sometimes called "clocks" or "blowballs".

global-18-nettle.title: Urtica dioica
global-18-nettle.description: Species of flowering plant in the family Urticaceae
global-18-nettle.extract: Urtica dioica, often known as common nettle, burn nettle, stinging nettle, nettle leaf, or just a nettle or stinger, is a herbaceous perennial flowering plant in the family Urticaceae. Originally native to Europe, much of temperate Asia and western North Africa, it is now found worldwide.

global-19-yarrow.title: Achillea millefolium
global-19-yarrow.extract: Achillea millefolium, commonly known as yarrow or common yarrow, is a flowering plant in the family Asteraceae. Growing to 1 metre tall, it is characterized by small whitish flowers, a tall stem of fernlike leaves, and a pungent odor.

global-20-linden-flower.title: Tilia
global-20-linden-flower.extract: Tilia species are mostly large, deciduous trees, reaching typically 20 to 40 m (65 to 130 ft) tall. They are hermaphroditic, having perfect flowers with both male and female parts, pollinated by insects. The flowers are small and symmetrical, with five sepals and five petals; they are strongly scented and attractive to bees.

global-21-hawthorn-european.title: Crataegus
global-21-hawthorn-european.description: Genus of flowering plants in the rose family Rosaceae
global-21-hawthorn-european.extract: Crataegus, commonly called hawthorn, quickthorn, thornapple, May-tree, whitethorn, Mayflower or hawberry, is a genus of several hundred species of shrubs and trees in the family Rosaceae, native to temperate regions of the Northern Hemisphere in Europe, Asia, North Africa and North America. The name "hawthorn" was originally applied to the species native to northern Europe, especially the common h

global-22-ginkgo-china.title: Ginkgo biloba
global-22-ginkgo-china.extract: Ginkgo biloba, commonly known as ginkgo, also known as the maidenhair tree, and often misspelled "gingko" is a species of gymnosperm tree native to East Asia. It is the last living species in the order Ginkgoales, which first appeared over 290 million years ago. Fossils similar to the living species, belonging to the genus Ginkgo, extend back to the Middle Jurassic epoch about 170 million years ag

global-23-bilberry.title: Vaccinium myrtillus
global-23-bilberry.extract: Vaccinium myrtillus is a holarctic species of shrub with edible fruit of blue color, known by the common names bilberry, blaeberry, wimberry, and whortleberry. It is more precisely called common bilberry or blue whortleberry to distinguish it from other Vaccinium relatives.

global-24-mullein.title: Verbascum thapsus
global-24-mullein.extract: Verbascum thapsus, the great mullein, greater mullein or common mullein, is a species of mullein native to Europe, northern Africa, and Asia, and introduced in the Americas, Australia and New Zealand.

global-25-black-seed.title: Nigella sativa
global-25-black-seed.description: Species of flowering plant in the buttercup family
global-25-black-seed.extract: Nigella sativa is an annual flowering plant in the family Ranunculaceae, native to western Asia, and eastern Europe. It is naturalised over parts of Europe, northern Africa, and east to Myanmar. It is used as a spice in various food preparations.

global-26-frankincense.title: Boswellia sacra
global-26-frankincense.extract: Boswellia sacra, also known as Boswellia carteri and others, and commonly called the frankincense tree or the olibanum tree, is a tree in the genus Boswellia, in the Burseraceae family, from which frankincense, a resinous dried sap, is harvested.

global-27-myrrh.title: Commiphora myrrha
global-27-myrrh.extract: Commiphora myrrha, called myrrh, Somali myrrh, herabol myrrh, common myrrh, is a tree in the family Burseraceae. It is one of the primary trees used in the production of myrrh, a resin made from dried tree sap. The Commiphora myrrha tree is indigenous to Somalia, in the Somali regions of Ethiopia and Kenya, Djibouti, Eritrea and parts of the Arabian Peninsula.

global-28-henna.title: Lawsonia inermis
global-28-henna.extract: Lawsonia inermis, also known as hina, the henna tree, the mignonette tree, and the Egyptian privet, is a flowering plant and one of the only two species of the genus Lawsonia, with the other being Lawsonia odorata. It is used as a traditional medicinal plant. The species is named after the Scottish physician Isaac Lawson, a good friend of Linnaeus.

global-29-date-milk-traditional.title: Phoenix dactylifera
global-29-date-milk-traditional.extract: Phoenix dactylifera, known as date palm, is a flowering plant species in the palm family Arecaceae. Date milk is a traditional Middle Eastern beverage made from dates and milk, rich in natural sugars, vitamins, and minerals.

global-30-za-atar-blend-thyme-sumac-sesa.title: Za'atar
global-30-za-atar-blend-thyme-sumac-sesa.extract: Za'atar is a traditional Middle Eastern herb blend typically containing dried thyme, oregano, marjoram, toasted sesame seeds, sumac, and salt. It is used as a seasoning in Middle Eastern cuisine and has been used traditionally for its medicinal properties.

global-31-rooibos-south-africa.title: Rooibos
global-31-rooibos-south-africa.description: Species of plant in the family Fabaceae
global-31-rooibos-south-africa.extract: Rooibos, or Aspalathus linearis, is a broom-like member of the plant family Fabaceae that grows in South Africa's Fynbos biome. The leaves are used to make a caffeine-free herbal infusion that has been popular in Southern Africa for generations. Since the 2000s, rooibos has gained popularity internationally, with an earthy flavour and aroma that is similar to those of yerba mate or tobacco.

global-32-honeybush.title: Cyclopia
global-32-honeybush.extract: Cyclopia, also known as alobar holoprosencephaly, is the most extreme form of holoprosencephaly and is a congenital disorder characterized by the failure of the embryonic prosencephalon to properly divide the orbits of the eye into two cavities. Its incidence is 1 in 16,000 in born animals and 1 in 200 in miscarried fetuses.

global-33-african-potato.title: Hypoxis hemerocallidea
global-33-african-potato.extract: Hypoxis hemerocallidea, the African star grass or African potato, is a medicinal plant in the Hypoxidaceae family. It is native to southern Africa from South Africa as far north as Mozambique and Zimbabwe. This plant is the best known member of this genus.

global-34-sutherlandia.title: Lessertia frutescens
global-34-sutherlandia.extract: Lessertia frutescens is a southern African legume in the family Fabaceae. It is also known as cancer bush, balloon pea, Cape bladder pea, sutherlandia; and phetola in seTswana, and insiswa in isiZulu. It is a shrub with bitter, aromatic leaves, with red-orange flowers appearing in spring to mid-summer. These are followed by inflated, air-filled pods containing the seeds.

global-35-moringa-africa.title: Moringa oleifera
global-35-moringa-africa.extract: Moringa oleifera is a short-lived, fast-growing, drought-resistant tree of the family Moringaceae, native to northern India and used extensively in South and Southeast Asia. Common names include moringa, drumstick tree, horseradish tree, or malunggay.

global-36-baobab-fruit.title: Adansonia digitata
global-36-baobab-fruit.description: Species of plant native to Africa
global-36-baobab-fruit.extract: Adansonia digitata, the African baobab, is the most widespread tree species of the genus Adansonia, the baobabs, and is native to the African continent and the southern Arabian Peninsula. These are long-lived pachycauls; radiocarbon dating has shown at least one individual to be 1,275 years old. They are typically found in dry, hot savannas of sub-Saharan Africa, where they dominate the landscape

global-37-shea-butter.title: Vitellaria
global-37-shea-butter.extract: Vitellaria paradoxa, commonly known as shea tree, shi tree, or vitellaria, is a tree of the family Sapotaceae. It is the only species in the genus Vitellaria, and is indigenous to Africa.

global-38-hibiscus.title: Roselle (plant)
global-38-hibiscus.extract: Roselle is a species of flowering plant in the genus Hibiscus that is native to Africa, most likely West Africa. In the 16th and early 17th centuries it was spread to Asia and the West Indies, where it has since become naturalized in many places. The stems are used for the production of bast fibre and the dried cranberry-tasting calyces are commonly steeped to make a popular infusion known by many

global-39-sage-smudge.title: Salvia apiana
global-39-sage-smudge.extract: Salvia apiana, the Californian white sage, bee sage, or sacred sage is an evergreen perennial shrub that is native to the southwestern United States and northwestern Mexico, found mainly in the coastal sage scrub habitat of Southern California and Baja California, on the western edges of the Mojave and Sonoran deserts.

global-40-sweetgrass.title: Hierochloe odorata
global-40-sweetgrass.extract: Hierochloe odorata, known as sweetgrass or holy grass, is a perennial grass native to northern Europe and North America. It is used in traditional ceremonies by Indigenous peoples and has a sweet, vanilla-like scent when dried.

global-41-slippery-elm.title: Ulmus rubra
global-41-slippery-elm.extract: Ulmus rubra, the slippery elm, is a species of elm native to eastern North America. Other common names include red elm, gray elm, soft elm, moose elm, and Indian elm. It is closely related to the European wych elm (U. glabra), which has a very similar flower structure but lacks the pubescence over the seed.

global-42-goldenseal.title: Goldenseal
global-42-goldenseal.extract: Goldenseal, also called orangeroot or yellow puccoon, is a perennial herb in the buttercup family Ranunculaceae, native to North America. It may be distinguished by its thick, yellow knotted rootstock. The stem is purplish and hairy above ground and yellow below ground where it connects to the yellow rhizome. Goldenseal reproduces both clonally through the rhizome and sexually, with clonal divisio

global-43-kava.title: Kava
global-43-kava.extract: Kava or kava kava is a plant in the pepper family, native to the Pacific Islands. The name kava is from Tongan and Marquesan, meaning 'bitter'. Kava can refer to either the plant or a psychoactive beverage made from its root. The beverage is a traditional ceremonial and recreational drink from Polynesia, Micronesia, and Melanesia. Nakamals and kava bars exist in many countries. Traditional kava is

global-44-manuka-honey-nz.title: Mānuka
global-44-manuka-honey-nz.description: Species of flowering plant endemic to New Zealand
global-44-manuka-honey-nz.extract: Mānuka is a species of flowering plant in the myrtle family Myrtaceae, native to New Zealand and south-east Australia. Bees produce mānuka honey from its nectar.

global-45-kawakawa-m-ori.title: Piper excelsum
global-45-kawakawa-m-ori.extract: Piper excelsum of the pepper family (Piperaceae) and commonly known as kawakawa, is a small tree of which the subspecies P. excelsum subsp. excelsum is endemic to New Zealand; the subspecies P. e. subsp. psittacorum is found on Lord Howe Island, Norfolk Island and the Kermadec Islands.

global-46-lemongrass.title: Cymbopogon citratus
global-46-lemongrass.extract: Cymbopogon citratus, commonly known as West Indian lemon grass or simply lemon grass, is a tropical plant native to South Asia and Maritime Southeast Asia and introduced to many tropical regions.

global-47-pandan.title: Pandanus amaryllifolius
global-47-pandan.extract: Pandanus amaryllifolius is a tropical plant in the Pandanus (screwpine) genus, which is commonly known as pandan. It has fragrant leaves that are used widely for flavouring in the cuisines of Southeast Asia. It is also featured in some South Asian cuisines and in Hainanese cuisine from China.

global-48-kratom-thailand.title: Mitragyna speciosa
global-48-kratom-thailand.extract: Mitragyna speciosa is a tropical evergreen tree of the Rubiaceae family native to Southeast Asia. It is indigenous to Cambodia, Thailand, Indonesia, Malaysia, Myanmar, and Papua New Guinea, where its dark green, glossy leaves, known as kratom, have been used in herbal medicine since at least the 19th century. They have also historically been consumed via chewing, smoking, and as a tea. Kratom cont

global-49-galangal.title: Alpinia galanga
global-49-galangal.extract: Alpinia galanga, a plant in the ginger family, bears a rhizome used largely as an herb in Unani medicine and as a spice in Southeast Asian cookery. It is one of four plants known as "galangal". Its common names include greater galangal, lengkuas, and blue ginger.

global-50-betel-leaf-south.title: Betel
global-50-betel-leaf-south.extract: Betel is a species of flowering plant in the pepper family Piperaceae, native to Southeast Asia. It is an evergreen, dioecious vine, with glossy heart-shaped leaves and white catkins. Betel plants are cultivated for their leaves, which are most commonly used as flavoring for chewing areca nut in so-called betel quid, which is toxic and is associated with a wide range of serious health conditions.

global-51-cleavers-global-weed.title: Galium aparine
global-51-cleavers-global-weed.extract: Galium aparine, with common names including cleavers, clivers, catchweed, robin-run-the-hedge, goosegrass, and sticky willy, is an annual, herbaceous plant of the family Rubiaceae.

global-52-chickweed-global-weed.title: Stellaria media
global-52-chickweed-global-weed.extract: Stellaria media, chickweed, is an annual flowering plant in the family Caryophyllaceae. It is native to Eurasia and naturalized throughout the world, where it is a weed of waste ground, farmland and gardens. It is sometimes grown as a salad crop or for poultry consumption.

global-53-fig-mediterranean.title: Fig
global-53-fig-mediterranean.description: Species of flowering plant, or its fruit
global-53-fig-mediterranean.extract: The fig is the edible fruit of Ficus carica, a species of tree or shrub in the flowering plant family Moraceae, native to the Mediterranean region, and to western and southern Asia. It has been cultivated since ancient times and is now widely grown throughout the world. Ficus carica is the type species of the genus Ficus, which comprises over 800 tropical and subtropical plant species.

global-54-rosehip-global.title: Rosa canina
global-54-rosehip-global.extract: Rosa canina, the dog rose, is a variable climbing, wild rose species native to Europe, northwest Africa, and western Asia.

global-55-turkey-tail-global-mushroom.title: Trametes versicolor
global-55-turkey-tail-global-mushroom.extract: Trametes versicolor – also known as Coriolus versicolor and Polyporus versicolor – is a common polypore mushroom found throughout the world. Owing to its shape being similar to that of a wild turkey's tail feathers, T. versicolor is most commonly referred to as turkey tail.

global-56-chaga-northern-hemisphere.title: Inonotus obliquus
global-56-chaga-northern-hemisphere.extract: Inonotus obliquus, commonly called chaga, is a fungus in the family Hymenochaetaceae. It is parasitic on birch and other trees. The sterile conk is irregularly formed and resembles burnt charcoal. It is not the fruiting body of the fungus, but a sclerotium or mass of mycelium, mostly black because of a substantial amount of melanin. It is commonly marketed as a dietary supplement for various healt

global-57-clove-indonesia.title: Clove
global-57-clove-indonesia.extract: Cloves are the aromatic flower buds of a tree in the family Myrtaceae, Syzygium aromaticum. They are native to the Maluku Islands, or Moluccas, in Indonesia, and are commonly used as a spice, flavoring, or fragrance in consumer products, such as toothpaste, soaps, or cosmetics. Cloves are available throughout the year owing to different harvest seasons across various countries.

global-58-eucalyptus.title: Eucalyptus
global-58-eucalyptus.extract: Eucalyptus is a genus of over seven hundred species of flowering trees, shrubs or mallees in the myrtle family, Myrtaceae. Along with several other genera in the tribe Eucalypteae, including Corymbia, they are commonly known as eucalypts.

global-59-schisandra.title: Schisandra
global-59-schisandra.extract: Schisandra is a genus of climbing vines in the family Schisandraceae. The genus includes several species of woody vines which are native to the forests of Northern China and the Russian Far East.

global-60-lemon-balm.title: Lemon balm
global-60-lemon-balm.extract: Melissa officinalis, known as lemon balm, balm, common balm, or balm mint, is a herbaceous perennial in the mint family Lamiaceae, native to south-central Europe, the Mediterranean Basin, Iran, and Central Asia.
